beats.activity: Waking up, coffee, checking weather and work email
beats.reasoning: Slow Tuesday start after yesterday's energy high; grey drizzle outside means Eli stays indoors longer, sipping coffee and assessing the day. Low energy (45/100) carries forward as caution.

beats.activity: Driving to Bellevue via I-5 north, avoiding SR-520 closure
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Eli leaves early to avoid the crunch, taking I-5 north to I-405 south instead. Adds 15 min buffer but keeps him ahead of snarled cross-lake traffic.

beats.activity: Data analysis work: Q2 reports and pipeline review
beats.reasoning: Steady rain until 2pm keeps Eli anchored at desk; routine Tuesday work rhythm, no spontaneous detours like yesterday. Focuses on reports before lunch window.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby sandwich shop, eating at desk
beats.reasoning: Rain still falling; Eli grabs a quick indoor lunch rather than venturing to food trucks. Stays close to work, conservative mood fitting the grey weather and low energy carryover.

beats.activity: Afternoon data work: deep-dive into Q2 pipeline metrics
beats.reasoning: Rain clears around 2pm; Eli stays focused at work through afternoon. No spontaneous urges today—yesterday's hooky feeling has worn off, and the drizzle discouraged wandering.

beats.activity: Driving home via I-405 south to I-5, traffic lighter after rush hour
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure now ended; Eli still takes I-405 south to I-5 (familiar route). Leaves at 5:30pm to miss peak congestion, home by 6:30pm.

beats.activity: Grocery run: Safeway for staples and food bank prep supplies
beats.reasoning: Eli stops at familiar neighborhood Safeway to restock; rain has cleared, temperature mild (58F). Quick errand before settling in for the evening.

beats.activity: Preparing dinner, organizing camera gear and negatives for upcoming photo walk
beats.reasoning: Low-energy evening: simple dinner, then back to hobby. Yesterday's photo gear inspiration carries over; Eli sorts negatives and preps for weekend shoot, a quiet ritual that fits his routine-bound nature.

beats.activity: Leisure: watching a film on his old projector, reviewing contact sheets
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening wind-down; Eli settles into favorite activity—film and photography—before bed. Low energy and grey day suggest early, restful night ahead.
Long, restorative sleep after a routine Tuesday. Yesterday's high energy has settled; Eli needs the rest before Wednesday.

diary: Grey morning, coffee tasted better than usual—maybe because the rain made everything feel slower. Checked the news about the SR-520 closure and decided early to take I-5 instead; turned out to be the right call. The office was quiet, everyone hunched over their screens. Spent most of the day buried in Q2 pipeline metrics, the kind of work that makes time disappear. Grabbed a sandwich at my desk around noon, didn't even taste it. After the drive home—mercifully smooth, the worst of the traffic had cleared—I stopped at Safeway to pick up some extra canned goods for the food bank. Something about rainy days makes me want to help. Evening was mine again. Organized my camera gear, sorted through old negatives, and watched an old film on the projector. The kind of night that reminds me why I still love this work, this gear, this quiet space to just be.
thought_process: The weather report hit me first thing—I've learned that Seattle rain and bridge closures mean chaos, so I pivoted early. Sticking to I-5 felt like the smart move, and it paid off. Work was absorbing but monotonous; I leaned into the data deep-dive because it's what I'm good at, even if it left me a bit drained. The grocery stop surprised me—I wasn't planning it initially, but the grey day and steady rain sparked something in me about community, about giving back. It felt necessary. By evening, I was ready to retreat into my hobbies: the camera work, the old negatives, the projector. That's where I recharge. The day had a rhythm to it, not exciting, but solid and grounded.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
